name: Montana Pharmacy Technician License
description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Montana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

address.description:
301 South Park
PO Box 200513
Helena MT 59620-0513

renewalCycle: Annual renewal window May 2 to June 30

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Montana pharmacy technician license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Montana Board of Pharmacy lists a $35 application fee for a certified pharmacy technician. The active renewal fee is $30.
Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does Montana have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Montana. To work in Montana, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Montana?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Montana Board of Pharmacy does not publish a public pharmacy technician processing timeline on its licensing page.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Montana while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Contact the Montana Board of Pharmacy to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in Montana.
